key_insight: A fault-tolerant, literature-grounded LLM pipeline can autonomously move from corpus mining to a publishable computational-physics manuscript while avoiding hallucinated science by repeatedly confronting its methods against external references.
engineering_relevance: The work suggests a practical architecture for high-stakes scientific automation: isolate sessions, enforce grounding checkpoints, and use redundancy plus adversarial review to make autonomous research more reliable.
method: Map a large arXiv corpus to identify a research direction.
Reproduce published references to calibrate methodology before novel computations.
Run fresh-context, multi-phase sessions with shared on-disk state and adversarial review for fault tolerance.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports an end-to-end run over 11,083 papers, 47 fresh-context sessions, 2,162 literature-consultation events, and three substantive physics findings in one case study.
watch_item: Results may depend on careful human intervention during reproduction failures, so portability to other domains and the reliability of fully autonomous calibration remain concerns.
paper_title: Grounded autonomous research: a fault-tolerant LLM pipeline from corpus to manuscript in frontier computational physics
